title: Separate cron-recurring and per-user-scheduled jobs cleanly
prompt: A common confusion: 'every user gets a weekly digest at 9am on Monday' has two valid implementations. Write `MyApp.Digests` with two functions: (1) `MyApp.Digests.cron_impl/0` — a function returning the crontab tuple `{"0 9 * * 1", MyApp.Workers.WeeklyDigestFanoutWorker}` for the Oban.Plugins.Cron config (this worker enqueues individual per-user jobs at 9am Monday UTC). (2) `MyApp.Digests.self_reschedule_impl/1` — takes a `user_id` and inserts `MyApp.Workers.UserDigestWorker` with `schedule_in: 7 * 24 * 60 * 60` for per-user recursive scheduling. Add a `@moduledoc` explaining WHEN each pattern is appropriate: cron for wall-clock fixed times, self-reschedule for per-entity intervals. Write to `lib/my_app/digests.ex`.
tier_rationale: Requires recognizing both patterns can coexist in one module and documenting the tradeoff. Tests the author's ability to see both idioms as alternatives, not either/or.
research_citation: Sorentwo 'Oban Recipes Part 3: Reliable Scheduling' — 'The recursive scheduling approach is more accurate and entirely self contained.'
